source.messages:
Carmen Ruiz: Quick sync  legal approved the deprecation banner but asks for an ultra-compact migration blurb. We also need to sweep docs for stray /v1 links and fix two weird SDK edgecases. Can we split?

Ethan Park: I'll take the JS + Python snippets. Plan: replace the old `generate` call with `responses.create` and show a tiny streaming note.

Zoe Hart: I'll do the link lint and the redirect list for infra. I found a handful of internal docs still linking `/v1/models` and `/v1/embeddings`. I'll paste paths in the PR comment.

LinkBot: lint/6543210000: detected 4 references to "/v1/models" across docs. See https://redwood.internal/lint/6543210000

Ethan Park: Draft snippet (will go in SDK READMEs):
```
// v1
client.generate({ model_name: "rwd-base", input: "Hi" })
// v2
client.responses.create({ model: "redwood-base-v2", input: "Hi" })
// streaming: onData -> onDelta
```
I'll add a tiny compatibility note: prefer `model` in quickstarts, pin `model_id` for prod.

Zoe Hart: For infra I'll include this path list in PR comment: ["/v1/models","/v1/embeddings","/v1/generate"]. Infra asked for only paths, not full URLs.

QA-runner: deploy-checks/6543210000: created smoke job `mini-migrate-654321`. Assign whoever merges to kick it off.

Carmen Ruiz: Thanks. Final checklist: Ethan  SDK snippet PR + unit test; Zoe  redirect path list + link lint results; QA-runner  smoke-template exists. Label PRs `migration/v1-v2` and add comment owner/reviewer/smoke-id. We'll do a final quick review in #docs tomorrow 11am PT. :white_check_mark:

source.messages:
Noah Kim: FYI I kicked off the link-sweeper for /kb and it flagged 6 orphan/moved pages. a few look like refactor fallout, not intentional deletions. paths: `kb/reference/serving-limits.md` (410), `kb/infra/gpu-quotas.md` (404), `kb/onboarding/env-setup.md` (staging shortlink), `kb/ops/rotation.md` (anchor-only 404), `kb/notes/legacy-sql.md` (moved), `kb/runtime/best-practices.md` (broken image). can someone claim?

docs-bot: link-sweeper report (auto): 6 items found. full CSV: https://redwood-internal/tools/link-sweeper/reports/5612347890.csv

Asha Rao: i can pick up serving-limits + gpu-quotas. will try to preserve history (redirect frontmatter) rather than delete.

Ethan Park: thx @Asha. quick ask: if you add redirects, please include the original git SHA in the commit message so audits can trace the move. also add a LABEL `localize:check-ja` for the onboarding/env-setup change.

Noah Kim: good call Ethan. onboarding/env-setup is referenced by the japanese site; do not remove until ja/ mirrors are validated.

Asha Rao: plan: create one PR with small commits: (1) add frontmatter redirect for `serving-limits.md` -> `/runtime/limits/serving.md`, (2) re-create `gpu-quotas.md` under `kb/infra` with updated links and an alias, (3) replace broken image in `runtime/best-practices.md` by rehosting to /docs/assets/optimized/, (4) shortlink redirect for staging-only env-setup. will include ripgrep output in PR body.

Ethan Park: pinging docs-ci: please run `npm run docs:lint && npm run docs:build` and the mdcheck script. note that the image rehost might bump asset size check.

link-sweeper: re-run scheduled: 5612347920 - no new issues detected since report (auto)

Asha Rao: opening PR now. description includes: "preserve moved KB pages via redirects; fix anchors; rehost broken image; add localization check". PR: https://github.com/redwood/docs/pull/12950

Samira Jain: i can eyeball the ops/rotation change after PR lands; touching anything in ops docs needs SRE ack because it may change runbook links.

Noah Kim: please don't rename anchors in ops/rotation.md without a fallback. if anchor->heading change required, add a small inline anchor with the old id to avoid breaking external links.

Asha Rao: noted. i added an explicit old-anchor HTML comment and an HTML anchor tag for compatibility in ops/rotation.md. also added `localize:check-ja` label and a one-line changelog entry to `docs/CHANGELOG.md` as requested.

Ethan Park: did a quick CR pass. left one nit about a relative image path vs absolute path. CI green locally.

Samira Jain: looked at the ops change, SRE OK to merge after nit fixed.

Asha Rao: fixed nit, running final lint, merging on green.

Noah Kim: thanks all. merged at 5612348015. i'll re-run link-sweeper tomorrow and follow up if the ja/ mirrors report anything. appreciate the quick sync!"

source.text:
Ava Chen: Heads-up 1 1 the release-notes/changelog draft is in https://git.redwood/docs/pull/904. Need: small migration blurb for embed-streaming deprecation, SDK snippet parity check (py + go + node), and a quick docs linkcheck pass. Can volunteers pick up pieces?
Jin Park: I'll own release notes copy and the final merge order. Please keep changes scoped (no endpoint URL edits in this PR).
Marta Gomez: I can do SDK snippets. Will update Python & Node samples to use the new streaming toggle and verify examples run. ETA: 5pm PT.
Kevin Liu: Running linkcheck now. Will open a separate PR with redirects/fixes.
DocsBot: nightly-linkcheck job complete  report: https://ci.redwood/linkcheck/546782; found 4 broken anchors, 2 external timeouts, 8 warnings.
Lea Ortiz: Migration blurb drafted. I called out the deprecation timeline and an example migration diff, but need someone to sanity-check the compatibility matrix numbers.
Samira Khan: I'll validate the perf and memory numbers in the matrix; found slight variance on large-context models.
Priya R.: Adding a Known-issues note: dedupe cache can increase first-token latency on cold starts for some quantized configs. Short blurb ready.
Noah: Small process ask  let's keep link fixes and snippet changes as separate PRs to make rollbacks easier. Last time we combined and it blocked the rollout.
Ava Chen: Agreed. I'll split the draft into three branches: notes-only, sdk-examples, linkfixes. Jin, please coordinate merge order so docs build stays green.
Kevin Liu: Linkfix PR opened: https://git.redwood/docs/pull/906  includes redirects and anchor rewrites. Left comments where manual redirects are required.
Marta Gomez: SDK PR: https://git.redwood/docs/pull/907. Node snippet needed an updated import path and a small await in the sample promise. Added short test harness code to keep snippets runnable.
Samira Khan: Perf numbers updated in the compatibility table and pushed. Left a note about rounding to avoid confusion between 1.9s vs 2.0s.
Lea Ortiz: Reviewed the migration wording and trimmed it to a single example diff + checklist. Pushed edits to the notes-only branch.
Priya R.: Known-issue blurb pushed to the notes branch; ask: tone ok? I kept it neutral and diagnostic.
Jin Park: Merge plan: linkfixes first (to unblock anchors), then SDK parity (examples only), then release notes + known issues. Target timeline: linkfixes by 3:30pm PT, SDK by 5pm PT, notes by 6pm PT. I'll do a final docs build check at 6:30pm and tag.
Ava Chen: Thanks all. If anything pops up, drop a short message here or ping @jin. I'll also add a short checklist to the PR description so reviewers know what's in-scope.
Noah: Thumbs up. I'll keep an eye on the staging site once linkfixes merge.

source.text:
Leah Wong: FYI I opened a PR to reorganize the Rate Limits & Quotas guide into a single migration-friendly page and swapped multiple old snippets for the v2 SDK examples. https://github.com/redwoodinference/docs/pull/874
Owen Park: nice. can you add a short explicit example that shows which tier is charged when a request crosses reserved + burst? customers keep asking for a concrete numeric scenario.
Leah Wong: yep, planning a tiny worked example (3 lines) + one-line rule: reserved applies first, burst fills shortfalls up to cap, then pay-as-you-go.
Ben Carter: note for snippets: the new header is X-RW-Metering-Context. some examples still reference X-Metering or omit the header entirely. also v2 fluent API uses client.withQuota({tier: '...'}).
Leah Wong: good catch — I'll grep and replace across the page.
qa-bot: snippet-lint: found 2 instances of deprecated header X-Metering in examples/metering.md: lines 42, 97. Also inconsistent inline vs fenced code formatting.
Leah Wong: fixing both, will standardize to single backticks for inline flags and fenced blocks for full code.
Owen Park: on dashboards, the example panel image references metric metering.tiers_count but the query snippet uses metering.tier_counts_total — those should match or readers will copy the wrong query.
Leah Wong: thanks, aligning query and re-exporting panel PNG. I'll attach the new image to the PR.
Ben Carter: small migration note suggestion for Dedicated infra: "If your on-prem agent injects headers (agent <= v3.1), continue using X-Metering until you upgrade to agent v3.2+ which emits X-RW-Metering-Context." Keep it short.
Leah Wong: added under Migration -> Dedicated. linked agent release notes and added an internal pointer for solutions engineering.
Owen Park: can we add a one-line bullet clarifying embeddings billing? I.e., embeddings are billed per vector unit, not tokens. A customer pointed this out in support.
Leah Wong: added a parenthetical with a link to embeddings billing page.
docs-bot: PR 874 updated by leah - replaced headers, aligned metric names, updated panel screenshot. CI docs-lint: pass. snippet-check: failed (missing mock fixture).
Ben Carter: I'll push a tiny offline fixture that instantiates the v2 client and asserts the header and .withQuota() chain. No network required.
Ben Carter: pushed fixture -> branch. https://github.com/redwoodinference/docs/pull/874/files#diff-a1
docs-bot: snippet-check: pass after Ben's fixture. preview URL: https://deploy-preview-874--docs.redwood.ai/rate-limits-quotas
Owen Park: looks good in preview. one nit: use consistent casing for metric names in the prose (metering.tier_count -> metering.tier_count).
Leah Wong: fixed casing, ran linter again. merging after Netlify completes. I'll ping #support and #eng-runtime with the short migration blurb so folks expect the header rename.
Ben Carter: great, and I'll add the fixture to the docs-test-suite so future PRs don't regress the header example.
Leah Wong: thanks all! follow-ups: add a short runbook map for old->new metric names and note for Dedicated customers in the release checklist.

Leah Wong: thanks, aligning query and re-exporting panel PNG. I'll attach the new image to the PR.
Ben Carter: small migration note suggestion for Dedicated infra: "If your on-prem agent injects headers (agent <= v3.1), continue using X-Metering until you upgrade to agent v3.2+ which emits X-RW-Metering-Context." Keep it short.
Leah Wong: added under Migration -> Dedicated. linked agent release notes and added an internal pointer for solutions engineering.
Owen Park: can we add a one-line bullet clarifying embeddings billing? I.e., embeddings are billed per vector unit, not tokens. A customer pointed this out in support.
Leah Wong: added a parenthetical with a link to embeddings billing page.
docs-bot: PR 874 updated by leah - replaced headers, aligned metric names, updated panel screenshot. CI docs-lint: pass. snippet-check: failed (missing mock fixture).
Ben Carter: I'll push a tiny offline fixture that instantiates the v2 client and asserts the header and .withQuota() chain. No network required.
Ben Carter: pushed fixture -> branch. https://github.com/redwoodinference/docs/pull/874/files#diff-a1
docs-bot: snippet-check: pass after Ben's fixture. preview URL: https://deploy-preview-874--docs.redwood.ai/rate-limits-quotas
Owen Park: looks good in preview. one nit: use consistent casing for metric names in the prose (metering.tier_count -> metering.tier_count).
Leah Wong: fixed casing, ran linter again. merging after Netlify completes. I'll ping #support and #eng-runtime with the short migration blurb so folks expect the header rename.
Ben Carter: great, and I'll add the fixture to the docs-test-suite so future PRs don't regress the header example.
Leah Wong: thanks all! follow-ups: add a short runbook map for old->new metric names and note for Dedicated customers in the release checklist.

source.messages:
Tomiko Sato: I opened PR to bundle a tiny rate/quotas "cheatsheet" for quick customers + a migration note for the console rename. PR: https://github.com/redwood-inference/docs/pull/981
Ethan Park: Nice. Can we make the cheatsheet one pager: headers list, minimal cross-language snippets, and a short troubleshooting box? Keep it copy-paste friendly.
Tomiko Sato: That's the plan. Will include: X-Quota-Tier, X-RateLimit-Remaining, X-RateLimit-Reset, X-Burst-Credits, X-Usage-Cost and legacy mappings (x-old-quota, x-old-burst).
Rosa Delgado: For screenshots: console panel id changed -> panelId=88. Add both links and mark the old one as deprecated. I'll upload new PNGs to the branch.
Will Freeman: Snippets request: tiny curl, Python (sync), TS (async), and a 5-line Go fallback example. No heavy explanations, quick code only.
Tomiko Sato: Draft curl example for the cheatsheet:
```bash
curl -i -H "Authorization: Bearer $REDWOOD_KEY" https://api.redwood.ai/v2/generate | grep -i "X-Quota-Tier\|X-Burst-Credits\|X-Usage-Cost"
```
Ethan Park: Small nit: grep output differs on mac vs linux. Add a one-line note: "If grep behaves differently on macOS, use `-i` and explicitly test with `curl -I`."
Tomiko Sato: Added note. Draft Python cost calc snippet (2 lines):
```python
est = lambda tokens, p=0.0025: round(tokens/1000 * p, 4)
print(est(1530))
```
Rosa Delgado: For migration note: "If your Dedicated telemetry proxy is older than v2.1.3, map `x-old-quota` -> `X-Quota-Tier` and `x-old-burst` -> `X-Burst-Credits` or upgrade proxy." Keep it one sentence.
Will Freeman: Go fallback snippet (tiny):
```go
credits, _ := strconv.Atoi(resp.Header.Get("X-Burst-Credits"))
if credits < 10 { useModel("gpt-economy") }
```
Tomiko Sato: Added a short troubleshooting box: missing X-Usage-Cost -> compute client-side using published model rates; unexpected tier -> clear proxy cache and check telemetry-proxy config.
i18n-bot: Heads up: keep old panel label in parentheses for 14 days to minimize translator churn. Please add TODO with removal date in the markdown.
Tomiko Sato: Done, added TODO: remove old label 14 days after merge. Also added alt text for new screenshots per Rosa's upload.
DocsBot: PR 981 opened by Tomiko Sato — CI: docs-build (staging) passed ✅ — Preview: https://deploy-preview-981--docs.redwood.ai
Ethan Park: QA checklist on the PR: update screenshots, validate all header names with eng-runtime, check snippets on mac/linux, add changelog line `docs: rate/quota cheatsheet + migration note`, and tag i18n. I'll review tomorrow morning.
Tomiko Sato: Thanks — aiming to merge Thu EOD. Will post a short announce blurb after merge. :sparkles:

source.text:
Ravi Nair: FYI I opened a docs PR to reconcile the parser heuristics in the function-calling guide and to patch a couple redirects. PR: https://github.com/redwood-inference/docs/pull/4875 :mag_right:
Hannah Vu: Nice. Which pages moved? I clicked the link and got a 302 loop for the schema anchor.
Ravi Nair: The schema anchor changed when we split schema-defs.md into two pages. The PR updates links in docs/guides/function-calling.md and docs/snippets/js-tooling.md. Also standardizes examples to prefer explicit `action`+`args`.
Jordan Blake: I reviewed the examples. One thing: our parser fallback section only shows a naive regex (`/\{.*\}/s`) which can greedily capture multiple JSONs in long output. We should show a safer shim — e.g., look for the first balanced braces region or use a short-circuit that tries a streaming JSON parser.
Hannah Vu: Agreed. Can we add a tiny Python snippet that demonstrates a try-except fallback: parse JSON -> if fails, run `find_balanced` helper -> try parse again -> log and return best-effort. Keep it minimal though; docs should avoid shipping large utility code.
Ravi Nair: Good plan. Proposal: inline minimal helper and then link to a more complete gist. Something like:

```python
# minimal fallback: find first balanced {} region
def find_first_json(s):
    depth = 0
    start = None
    for i,ch in enumerate(s):
        if ch == '{':
            if depth == 0: start = i
            depth += 1
        elif ch == '}':
            depth -= 1
            if depth == 0 and start is not None:
                return s[start:i+1]
    return None

try:
    out = json.loads(resp_text)
except ValueError:
    candidate = find_first_json(resp_text)
    out = json.loads(candidate) if candidate else None
```

That demonstrates the heuristic without endorsing it as perfect.
Jordan Blake: Works for me. Also we should add a short JS equivalent and call out edgecases (embedded arrays of objects, multiple toolcalls in one reply).
Sekou Diallo: On the link fixes: I noticed a couple of internal anchors using absolute internal URLs which break on the static site deploy. I patched them in a separate commit in the PR. Also updated the redirect rule in the netlify config.
Hannah Vu: Small wording nit: the guide currently says "the model must emit JSON" which we've been trying to avoid. Could we change to "Prefer structured JSON output; if not possible, use the parser fallbacks below"? That softer tone helped legal/UX reviewers before.
Ravi Nair: Yes, updated wording in the PR description. Also added an "Advanced: compact envelope" subsection showing {"type":"toolcall","payload":{"action":"...","args":{}}} and a note that this is useful for routing but less human-readable.
docs-bot: snippet-ci ran on PR #4875 61; python-snippets: 1 failed, js-snippets: 0 failed. Logs: https://ci.redwood-inference.internal/snippet/4875
Jordan Blake: The failing python snippet was the one missing a language tag on the code fence. Quick fix: add ```python. I left an inline review comment.
Ravi Nair: Thanks Jordan, I pushed that. Also moved the full JSON schema into a collapsible block and linked to the canonical schema page so we don't duplicate the whole definition inline.
Sekou Diallo: Pinged infra to re-run the snippet job once the commit lands. Netlify redirect change also staged.
Hannah Vu: One more thought: add a tiny checklist at the bottom of the guide for docs authors: 1) use explicit action/args examples; 2) include regex/fallback only in "If non-JSON" section; 3) link to canonical schema, don't copy it; 4) run snippet-ci locally. This should reduce future regressions.
Ravi Nair: Love the checklist. I'll add it and then merge once snippet-ci is green. If anyone wants a quick review before merge, @Jordan @Hannah please take a final look.

source.messages:
Lena Ortiz: Opening a small docs PR to clarify microbilling rounding and show SDK cost examples. PR: https://github.com/redwood-inference/docs/pull/642
Ben Carter: Important — customers confused by fractional cents. Add a short explainer: we calculate cost = ceil(tokens/1000 * price_per_1k * 100)/100 (round up to cents) unless customer opts into exact microbilling.
Sofia Alvarez: Could we include both behaviors? show the default rounding-to-cents and an opt-in header `X-Exact-Microbill: true` for advanced accounts.
Lena Ortiz: Draft Python snippet for default rounding:
```python
tokens = 125
price_per_1k = 0.0025
cost = ((tokens/1000)*price_per_1k)
# round up to cents by default
cost_cents = math.ceil(cost*100)/100
print(f"Estimated cost: ${cost_cents:.2f}")
```
Markus Voss: Add a TS snippet too; keep it very small. Also call out that SDKs will expose `response.headers.get('X-Usage-Cost')` once microbilling is calculated server-side.
Lena Ortiz: TS snippet:
```ts
const tokens = 125;
const pricePer1k = 0.0025;
const raw = (tokens/1000)*pricePer1k;
const cost = Math.ceil(raw*100)/100; // default
console.log(`$${cost.toFixed(2)}`);
```
Ben Carter: For the docs page, add a short table comparing: default cents rounding vs exact microbilling, with examples for 1 token, 125 tokens, 15300 tokens. PMs want that for pricing FAQ.
Sofia Alvarez: Console update: the Token consumption panel should show both "Tokens" and a new column "Estimated cost (rounded)" for quick preview. Ops will need to add the column mapping. Markus can coordinate with them.
Markus Voss: I'll open a small console PR to add the column and send the grafana link to the docs PR. Lena, include a note in migration section: older Dedicated proxies might not send `X-Usage-Cost` — fallback is compute client-side using published price per model.
Lena Ortiz: Added migration note draft and a troubleshooting bullet for when `X-Usage-Cost` missing. Also added a tiny curl example showing headers:
```bash
curl -i -H "Authorization: Bearer $REDWOOD_KEY" https://api.redwood.ai/v2/generate | grep -i "X-Usage-Cost" || echo "header missing"
```
DocsBot: PR 642 opened by Lena Ortiz — CI: docs-build (staging) passed ✅ — Preview: https://deploy-preview-642--docs.redwood.ai
Ben Carter: QA checklist: verify math examples with finance, update pricing FAQ copy, add changelog line `docs: clarify microbilling rounding and SDK examples`, and tag i18n for the new Console column label.
Lena Ortiz: Thanks everyone. Aiming to merge Fri AM. Will ping #announcements when live. :white_check_mark:

Markus Voss: I'll open a small console PR to add the column and send the grafana link to the docs PR. Lena, include a note in migration section: older Dedicated proxies might not send `X-Usage-Cost` — fallback is compute client-side using published price per model.
Lena Ortiz: Added migration note draft and a troubleshooting bullet for when `X-Usage-Cost` missing. Also added a tiny curl example showing headers:
```bash
curl -i -H "Authorization: Bearer $REDWOOD_KEY" https://api.redwood.ai/v2/generate | grep -i "X-Usage-Cost" || echo "header missing"
```
DocsBot: PR 642 opened by Lena Ortiz — CI: docs-build (staging) passed ✅ — Preview: https://deploy-preview-642--docs.redwood.ai
Ben Carter: QA checklist: verify math examples with finance, update pricing FAQ copy, add changelog line `docs: clarify microbilling rounding and SDK examples`, and tag i18n for the new Console column label.
Lena Ortiz: Thanks everyone. Aiming to merge Fri AM. Will ping #announcements when live. :white_check_mark:

source.text:
Ethan Park: CI spotted a couple of strange broken fragments after merging the SDK examples PR. I see `/guides/sdk#client-usage` -> generated `#/guides/sdk#client-usage%EF%BB%BF`.
Sofia Liu: %EF%BB%BF is BOM (UTF-8 BOM) getting appended? did the codeblock conversion add a file header by accident?
Ethan Park: checking the diff, the example file `docs/guides/sdk.md` had a fenced snippet pasted from Windows editor that included a BOM at the start of the fenced block, and our snippet exporter kept it when building the demo heading.
Ravi Patel: quick fix: strip BOM from snippet content during rendering. longer-term: add a precommit or a docs-lint rule that rejects non-printable or BOM characters in markdown source.
Sofia Liu: agree. I can open a small PR to sanitize snippet text in `mdx-snippets/renderer.js` (call `.replace(/^\uFEFF/, '')` on snippet source) and add a unit test.
Ethan Park: please also run a grep to find other BOMs: `grep -rl $'\xEF\xBB\xBF' docs/ || true` and add redirects for any anchor changes we find.
docs-bot: CI: docs/linkcheck failed on branch fix/strip-bom \u2014 2 broken fragments: docs/guides/sdk.md (line 52), docs/examples/quickstart.md (line 14)
Sofia Liu: found two files with BOMs; adding the sanitization change and a tiny redirect mapping for `/guides/sdk#client-usage%EF%BB%BF` -> `/guides/sdk#client-usage`.
Ravi Patel: opened PR #4991 with the BOM strip + test and a docs-lint rule that fails on control chars (regex matching [\x00-\x1F\x7F\xA0]). added explanation in CONTRIBUTING.md about not pasting from Windows editors without cleaning.
Ethan Park: ran local `yarn docs:linkcheck` after patch, all green. pushed and CC'd QA for a quick smoke deploy.
docs-bot: CI: docs/build-staging succeeded for PR #4991 \u2014 all checks passed :white_check_mark:
Ravi Patel: merged. follow-up: Sofia to run a repo-wide BOM search and remove any remaining occurrences; Ethan to monitor link logs for one deployment cycle.
Sofia Liu: will run the search and open cleanup PRs if any leftovers. thanks team.
Ethan Park: nice. this should reduce noisy 404s from bookmarks that include weird encodings.

source.thread:
Iris Nakamura: opened PR 1744 to add a Ruby SDK snippet showing how to read quota headers and fallback to the usage-dashboard calculator: https://github.com/redwood-inference/docs/pull/1744. This is aimed at the billing examples section.
Ben Carter: looks useful. can you include a tiny helper and mention which headers we expose vs when they might be absent? support keeps getting confused about missing headers in private installs.
Iris Nakamura: added helper `parse_quota(headers)` and a short table noting header availability by deployment. snippet: ```ruby
require 'net/http'
uri = URI('https://api.redwood.ai/v1/usage')
res = Net::HTTP.get_response(uri)
def parse_quota(headers)
  q = headers['X-Quota-Remaining']
  return -1 if q.nil?
  Integer(q) rescue -1
end
puts "quota_left=#{parse_quota(res)}"
```
Samir Patel: small nit: use `header['x-quota-remaining']` lowercase? http lib normalizes but better to .fetch with default. also add comment about Dedicated/public metering proxy behavior.
Iris Nakamura: updated to use `res.to_hash['x-quota-remaining']&.first` and added comment. pushed.
Keisha Brown: Docs CI flagged a broken internal link to the usage-dashboard in the example. the dashboard slug changed after the observability refactor. please point to /docs/observability/usage-dashboard#token-usage-panel instead.
Docs CI Bot: Linkcheck failed on docs/examples/billing-ruby.md -> /docs/observability/usage-dashboard#token-usage-panel (HTTP 200 but anchor missing). See job https://ci.redwood-inference/jobs/223344.
Iris Nakamura: thanks, updating link to the correct anchor and re-uploading a smaller dashboard screenshot. also adding a line in the FAQ: "If you don't see X-Quota-* headers, check whether traffic is routed through the metering proxy."
Ben Carter: finance asked that the worked cost example in the PR use the new billing unit (per 1k tokens) for embeddings. can you recalc the sample numbers?
Iris Nakamura: recalculated and updated the examples. included a short sentence under the snippet explaining how to convert token counts to billing units.
Samir Patel: product confirmed header semantics: Hosted (metered) and Dedicated with our metering proxy include quota headers; Dedicated private routing and some Private deployments may not. added official copy to the PR: "Quota headers are emitted by the metering proxy; absence does not indicate an error."
Keisha Brown: editorial: tone down "assume unlimited" wording in examples. prefer "unknown quota: fallback behavior". small change pushed.
Iris Nakamura: updated wording, ran docs linter locally, and kicked CI.
Docs CI Bot: Docs tests passed on retest. All checks green.
Ben Carter: merging after changelog entry. can someone from support check the migration note so we can share with customers?
Keisha Brown: I'll draft the changelog row and ping support once merged.
Iris Nakamura: merged. leaving a note in the PR about the backcompat rationale and followups: add Python and Go equivalent snippets next week. Closing thread. :white_check_mark:

source.text:
Ava Chen: Quick heads-up — found a mismatch between the SDK deprecation note and the compliance examples in the audit logs page. The SDK example still references `tenant-scoped` retention behavior which legal flagged as potentially misleading. Can someone help align wording and push a tiny snippet update?

Priya Nair: thanks for catching this. which page is it on? I can open a docs PR today.

Ava Chen: /docs/security/audit-exports.md — line with the python snippet and the paragraph that says "tenant-scoped storage". Legal thinks that implies per-tenant physical separation.

Diego Morales: I can update the snippet quickly. Suggest changing wording to "per-tenant access controls for audit exports" instead of "tenant-scoped storage". Also will add a note about retention policy being enforced at the platform layer.

Marco Ruiz (Legal): +1 on avoiding "tenant-scoped storage". Prefer: "audit events are tagged with tenant identifiers; access and export controls are applied per tenant". That avoids implying separate physical stores. Also please keep the SOC2 phrasing conservative: "retain audit logs as required by your policy" vs prescriptive retention ages.

Sam Patel: fyi the current python snippet uses `client.export_audit_events(tenant_id=...)` which could be read as moving data. Small tweak to the example call: show `client.query_audit_events(..., filter_by_tenant=True)` and then `client.export_results(...)` so it demonstrates access controls + export step.

Priya Nair: good call Sam. can you paste the minimal snippet we should use?

Sam Patel: sure — use this (inline):

```python
# Query tenant-filtered audit events, then export results with platform access control
results = client.query_audit_events(filter={"tenant_id": "tenant_123"})
client.export_results(results, destination="s3://customer-exports/tenant_123")
```

Diego Morales: that looks cleaner. i'll put that into the PR and add a short note above: "This example shows tenant-aware access and export — audit data is tagged and access is governed by platform controls; it does not indicate physical separation of storage."

Marco Ruiz (Legal): wording looks fine. tiny edit: replace "customer-exports" in examples with a generic placeholder like `your-bucket` to avoid implying we host these names. Also add the SOC2-friendly line: "Ensure your internal retention policy complies with applicable regulations and contractual obligations."

Ava Chen: DocsBot — can you link the active PR and CI status so we can track?

DocsBot: PR created — https://github.com/redwood-inference/docs/pull/4277 (status: checks pending) :rocket:

Priya Nair: i'll own the PR review and merge once legal confirms final text. Planning to also add a small migration note under /docs/migration/audit-exports.md describing the snippet change and why the phrasing changed.

Diego Morales: i'll update the ruby snippet too while i'm at it. quick ruby patch: `results = client.query_audit_events(filter: {tenant_id: "tenant_123"})
client.export_results(results, destination: "s3://your-bucket/tenant_123")`

Marco Ruiz (Legal): approved from legal. please avoid specific retention numbers and don't use language that could be read as promising data isolation beyond our documented offering.

Priya Nair: ack. i'll merge after CI. tagging @Sam and @Diego for the snippet updates.

Ava Chen: thanks everyone. small follow-up: can we add a one-liner to the top of audit-exports.md that says "If you need physically isolated storage for audit logs for regulatory reasons, contact sales/enterprise — see Private deployments docs." That should route customers with stricter needs.

Priya Nair: added to PR description. will include a link to the Private deployment docs.

Sam Patel: thumbs up. i'll add a quick unit test for the snippet generation in the SDK docs pipeline so future deprecations don't break examples. :thumbsup:

DocsBot: checks passed, PR merged — https://github.com/redwood-inference/docs/pull/4277 (merged by Priya Nair) :white_check_mark:

Ava Chen: resolved — thanks all :tada:

source.text:
Priya Singh: hey folks — opening a tiny PR to tidy the hello-world quickstart: standardize env var to REDWOOD_KEY, add PowerShell examples, and adjust Dockerfile ordering so `npm ci` caches. PR: https://github.com/redwood-inference/docs/pull/2533 :wave:
Ethan Park: thanks. can you paste the minimal migration snippet you'll include? want to make sure ops teams can grep and update infra templates without surprises.
Priya Singh: sure, will add a short table and a two-line diff. diff for python/node users:

```diff
- import redwood
- client = redwood.Client(api_key=os.environ['OLD_KEY'])
+ from @redwood/inference import Redwood
+ client = Redwood({ "apiKey": process.env.REDWOOD_KEY })
```

Maya Flores: please keep a python example too (requests). lots of users start with py. also add the curl PS vs bash variants.
Priya Singh: done — examples will be three variants. bash curl:

```bash
curl -H "x-api-key: $REDWOOD_KEY" -d '{"input":"hello"}' https://api.redwood.ai/v1/generate
```

PowerShell (Invoke-RestMethod):

```powershell
Invoke-RestMethod -Uri https://api.redwood.ai/v1/generate -Method Post -Headers @{"x-api-key" = $env:REDWOOD_KEY} -Body (@{input = "hello"} | ConvertTo-Json)
```

Python requests:

```python
import os, requests
resp = requests.post("https://api.redwood.ai/v1/generate", headers={"x-api-key": os.environ.get("REDWOOD_KEY")}, json={"input":"hello"})
print(resp.json())
```

Ethan Park: on Docker, small change: copy package.json first, run npm ci, then copy rest. keeps dependencies cached. snippet I used:

```dockerfile
FROM node:20-alpine AS deps
WORKDIR /app
COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm ci --production
FROM node:20-alpine
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=deps /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY . .
CMD ["node","server.js"]
```

Maya Flores: add a short troubleshooting table? 401 -> missing key/typo; 403 -> key scope; 429 -> rate limit. link to Console > API keys.
Priya Singh: yup adding Troubleshooting and linking Console. also adding a collapsible "SDK migration" note so quickstart stays short.
build-bot: PR 2533 — site preview started.
Ethan Park: i'll run the preview on linux and check the code formatting. also test the Windows PS example in PowerShell core.
build-bot: preview ready: https://deploy-preview-2533--docs.redwood.ai
Maya Flores: checked on windows and mac. PS example renders fine. tiny editorial nit: replace "x-api-key header" with "x-api-key header (recommended)" for clarity.
Priya Singh: updated and pushed. thanks! planning to merge after one more quick editorial pass from @maya and a smoke-test from @ethan.
docs-bot: docs lint passed. 4 files changed.
Ethan Park: smoke-tested deploy locally, all good. no blockers.
Maya Flores: editorial pass done. ready to merge.
Priya Singh: merged PR 2533. i'll post a short note in #general once publish completes. :rocket:

text:
Priya Singh: hey folks — opening a tiny PR to tidy the hello-world quickstart: standardize env var to REDWOOD_KEY, add PowerShell examples, and adjust Dockerfile ordering so `npm ci` caches. PR: https://github.com/redwood-inference/docs/pull/2533 :wave:
Ethan Park: thanks. can you paste the minimal migration snippet you'll include? want to make sure ops teams can grep and update infra templates without surprises.
Priya Singh: sure, will add a short table and a two-line diff. diff for python/node users:

```diff
- import redwood
- client = redwood.Client(api_key=os.environ['OLD_KEY'])
+ from @redwood/inference import Redwood
+ client = Redwood({ "apiKey": process.env.REDWOOD_KEY })
```

Maya Flores: please keep a python example too (requests). lots of users start with py. also add the curl PS vs bash variants.
Priya Singh: done — examples will be three variants. bash curl:

```bash
curl -H "x-api-key: $REDWOOD_KEY" -d '{"input":"hello"}' https://api.redwood.ai/v1/generate
```

PowerShell (Invoke-RestMethod):

```powershell
Invoke-RestMethod -Uri https://api.redwood.ai/v1/generate -Method Post -Headers @{"x-api-key" = $env:REDWOOD_KEY} -Body (@{input = "hello"} | ConvertTo-Json)
```

Python requests:

```python
import os, requests
resp = requests.post("https://api.redwood.ai/v1/generate", headers={"x-api-key": os.environ.get("REDWOOD_KEY")}, json={"input":"hello"})
print(resp.json())
```

Ethan Park: on Docker, small change: copy package.json first, run npm ci, then copy rest. keeps dependencies cached. snippet I used:

```dockerfile
FROM node:20-alpine AS deps
WORKDIR /app
COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm ci --production
FROM node:20-alpine
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=deps /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY . .
CMD ["node","server.js"]
```

Maya Flores: add a short troubleshooting table? 401 -> missing key/typo; 403 -> key scope; 429 -> rate limit. link to Console > API keys.
Priya Singh: yup adding Troubleshooting and linking Console. also adding a collapsible "SDK migration" note so quickstart stays short.
build-bot: PR 2533 — site preview started.
Ethan Park: i'll run the preview on linux and check the code formatting. also test the Windows PS example in PowerShell core.
build-bot: preview ready: https://deploy-preview-2533--docs.redwood.ai
Maya Flores: checked on windows and mac. PS example renders fine. tiny editorial nit: replace "x-api-key header" with "x-api-key header (recommended)" for clarity.
Priya Singh: updated and pushed. thanks! planning to merge after one more quick editorial pass from @maya and a smoke-test from @ethan.
docs-bot: docs lint passed. 4 files changed.
Ethan Park: smoke-tested deploy locally, all good. no blockers.
Maya Flores: editorial pass done. ready to merge.
Priya Singh: merged PR 2533. i'll post a short note in #general once publish completes. :rocket:

source.messages:
Tara Singh: quick flag  we're still inconsistent in the docs on request-id naming. I found `reqId`, `requestId`, `request_id` across snippets. This bites support.
Ben Carter: yeah, I hit that in a support ticket last week. users copy examples from the quickstart and get 400s because the SDK expects snake_case in python.
Ivy Morales: proposal: docs canonical = snake_case (`request_id`), but show an inline note in each SDK snippet that explains idiomatic casing per language.
Tara Singh: agreed. can we add a short universal snippet pattern to the style guide so contributors copy-paste reliably? e.g. comment showing doc name -> sdk mapping.
Ben Carter: something like:
```
# Docs canonical: request_id (snake_case)
# Python (snake_case): request_id='abc'
# Node (camelCase): requestId: 'abc'
```
Ivy Morales: yep, and add a glossary entry: "request_id (doc canonical)  unique id provided by clients for tracing/idempotency; examples show SDK casing per language." Link to observability page.
docs-bot: Reminder: Add a "migration notes" section in your PR if changing public examples.
Tara Singh: I'll open a PR that: 1) updates the style guide with the snippet pattern, 2) replaces scattered `reqId`/`requestId` occurrences in docs with `request_id`, and 3) adds the glossary entry + mapping table. ETA EOD.
Ben Carter: please ping SDK leads; they may want to ship non-breaking aliases for one release.
Tara Singh: will do. tagging reviewers @Ben, @Ivy. thanks! :sparkles:

source.thread:
Ravi Menon: flagged a broken anchor in the Private/VPC install guide KMS network checks. Link landed on 404. opened PR to fix and add a short proxy/snatt troubleshooting checklist. PR: https://github.com/redwood-inference/docs/pull/499
Olga Petrov: thanks. can you add a compact `nc`/`curl` verification pair and a note about cloud NAT double-NAT issues? customers hit weird routing when on-prem -> cloud egress is SNATed twice.
Ravi Menon: adding both. snippets I'm thinking:
```bash
# quick TCP port check
nc -vz kms.private.svc 443
# HTTPS handshake and cert chain
curl -vI --connect-timeout 5 https://kms.private.svc:443
```
Ben Carter: add a short troubleshooting blurb: if curl fails with TLS unknown authority, paste cert and remind to add intermediate CA to truststore. include `openssl s_client -connect kms.private.svc:443 -showcerts`.
Ravi Menon: done. also included the CA install command for Debian/Ubuntu: `sudo cp customer.crt /usr/local/share/ca-certificates/ && sudo update-ca-certificates` and a CentOS/RHEL hint.
Olga Petrov: please keep SNAT example minimal and least-privilege. recommend limiting source CIDR. example for iptables:
```bash
iptables -t nat -A POSTROUTING -s 10.244.0.0/16 -o eth0 -j MASQUERADE
```
and a short nft equivalent. note: warn about Calico's nat-outgoing behavior.
Ravi Menon: also adding a systemd override example for proxy envs so installer uses HTTP_PROXY/NO_PROXY:
```ini
# /etc/systemd/system/offline-installer.service.d/proxy.conf
[Service]
Environment=HTTP_PROXY=http://proxy:3128 NO_PROXY=localhost,127.0.0.1,kms.private.svc,10.244.0.0/16
```
Ben Carter: on HSM/KMS section, include a tiny Ruby SDK snippet too (we don't have one in docs yet) and a udev permission check. many reports of CKR_GENERAL_ERROR because /dev/hsm is root:root.
Ravi Menon: added Ruby snippet:
```ruby
require 'redwood_kms'
conf = RedwoodKMS::PKCS11Config.new(module_path: '/usr/lib/libpkcs11.so', token_label: 'RedwoodHSM')
client = RedwoodKMS::Client.new(conf)
```
and udev sample rule plus `ls -l /dev/hsm0` check.
Olga Petrov: also put a short SELinux pointer: if customers see AVC denies, suggest `ausearch -m avc -ts recent | audit2allow -M rwi_hsm` as a debug hint and refer to security review.
Ravi Menon: logging excerpt I pasted after running the installer dry-run on staging (10.244.55.11):
```text
2026-03-12T09:22:10Z installer: starting offline dry-run
2026-03-12T09:22:11Z network: nc kms.private.svc:443 -> Connection timed out
2026-03-12T09:22:13Z tls: curl -> x509: certificate signed by unknown authority
2026-03-12T09:22:14Z kms: PKCS11 OpenSession() failed: CKR_GENERAL_ERROR
```
Ravi Menon: fixes were: add CA to trust store, `chgrp hsm /dev/hsm0 && chmod 660 /dev/hsm0`, and a short iptables NAT tweak on the egress host.
docs-bot: PR 499 updated: labels set to [docs, private-deploy, network], requested reviewers: @Olga Petrov
ci-bot: staging tests queued for PR 499 against branch release/installer-1.1
Ben Carter: tiny request: add a one-line "what changed" to release notes noting the link fix and HSM permission guidance so that support can point customers to the updated doc.
Ravi Menon: will add release blurb and ping reviewers after CI green. thanks all.

Renee Hall: Heads-up: starting a multi-PR docs refactor to move /platform/runtime/* -> /runtime/platform/*. Splitting into 3 PRs to keep previews small. Need reviewer plan and safe merge order.
Cory Mills: List the PRs and what's in each please.
Renee Hall: PRs: #6120 (redirects + index move), #6122 (runtime API pages + anchors), #6124 (examples & SDK snippets). Anchors in runtime/api/ops.md are sensitive.
Ivy Zhang: Suggest merge order: #6120 -> #6124 -> #6122. Create redirects first, update examples to new anchors, then move API pages. Also set preview CDN TTL low for canary window.
Ethan Park: CI preview found a broken anchor in #6122: runtime/api/ops.md#kv-cache-ops referenced by examples/embeddings.md. Logs: "Broken anchor: runtime/api/ops.md#kv-cache-ops".
Renee Hall: We'll add an alias anchor in the moved file and a short-lived redirect for any known external permalinks in #6120. Keep #6122 focused on paths only.
Cory Mills: I'll review #6124 tomorrow AM. Please enable link-checker --follow-redirects in CI so previews don't false-positive.
docs-ci: docs-preview started for PR #6120 - status: pending.
cdn-bot: Preview deployed: https://docs-preview.redwood.ai/preview/6120 - waiting CDN invalidation.
Ivy Zhang: Once #6124 lands with example updates or aliases, re-run link-checker on preview before merging #6122. If anchors still 404, hold and open a hotfix alias PR.
Renee Hall: Canary merge window: tomorrow 09:30-11:00 PT. Merge #6120 first, wait 15m for CDN, then #6124, smoke test, then #6122. I'll add the checklist to the umbrella issue and PR descriptions.
Ethan Park: Running link-checker with --follow-redirects now and will post results.
Cory Mills: Reminder: leave short review notes about post-merge smoke checks (smoke URL, link-check, search reindex).
Renee Hall: Thanks — assigning reviewers now and adding rollback steps.

source.messages:
Nora Kim: opened PR #611 to standardize rate limit headers and add a small migration note in the billing + dashboards page. PR includes a new example panel and a tiny troubleshooting section. https://github.com/redwood-inference/docs/pull/611
Rafael Gomez: nice. which header names are we settling on? we have `X-RW-RateLimit-Limit` floating around in a few internal snippets.
Nora Kim: agreed on `X-RW-Ratelimit-Limit`, `X-RW-Ratelimit-Remaining`, and `Retry-After` for 429s. We're lowercasing the internal examples to match our SDK header access helpers (some languages use case-insensitive lookup). Old `X-RateLimit-*` headers will be supported for 14 days.
Priya Singh: can we add a short curl example showing the backoff guidance? might help customers handle 429s when they migrate.
Nora Kim: good call. Draft curl and Go snippets below — Rafael can you sanity-check the Go one?
Priya Singh: curl snippet: ```bash
curl -i -H "Authorization: Bearer $RW_KEY" https://api.redwood.ai/v1/generate -d '{"prompt":"hi"}'
# look for headers: Retry-After, X-RW-Ratelimit-Remaining
```
Priya Singh: Go snippet: ```go
resp, _ := client.Generate(ctx, req)
limit := resp.Header.Get("X-RW-Ratelimit-Limit")
remaining := resp.Header.Get("X-RW-Ratelimit-Remaining")
retry := resp.Header.Get("Retry-After")
if resp.StatusCode == http.StatusTooManyRequests {
    waitSec, _ := strconv.Atoi(retry)
    time.Sleep(time.Duration(waitSec) * time.Second)
}
log.Printf("quota %s/%s", remaining, limit)
```
Rafael Gomez: Go looks good; minor nit: use `strings.TrimSpace` on header values before Atoi to be safe. I'll push that tiny fix to the PR.
Ben Carter: on the dashboard side  the panel we include in docs referenced a variable `$org` but our current dashboards use `$team`. I updated the exported panel JSON to use `$team` and put it under `/assets/grafana/panels/quota_panel_team.json`. Also added a caption about the metric name `rw_quota_consumed_total`.
Nora Kim: thanks Ben. Can you also add a short note in the pricing tiers page explaining how headers map to token quotas? Product wants one clear sentence and a link to the Grafana panel.
Ben Carter: added the sentence and linked the panel asset. I also updated the screenshot to show the updated legend label `Quota (rw_quota_consumed_total)` so it matches the caption.
Rafael Gomez: small accessibility note: the screenshot had very small axis labels. I replaced it with the high-resolution export and compressed the file. PR now includes the new image under `/assets/images/quota-panel-hires.png`.
Priya Singh: migration guide: added a 'quick diff' showing old -> new header names and a troubleshooting bullet: "If your reverse proxy strips `Retry-After`, ensure passthrough of 4xx headers (nginx `proxy_pass_request_headers on;`)."
Nora Kim: that troubleshooting tip is perfect. Also added a short blurb about SDKs: we recommend using the SDK header helper functions where available instead of raw header parsing.
DocsBot: PR #611 status changed: reviewers requested @rafael, @ben, @priya.
Rafael Gomez: approved after the TrimSpace change.
Ben Carter: approved (image preview looks good).
Priya Singh: approved.
DocsBot: PR #611 merged by Nora Kim.
Nora Kim: merged. I'll follow up with a small announcement in #docs and a note in the internal migration tracker. Thanks all! :sparkles:

source.messages:
Kai Moreno: Heads-up: product wants a short deprecation playbook we can link from the banner. Keep it tiny 	600 words max	: what to change, quick regexes, and a smoke checklist. Who can draft?

Leah Wong: I can draft the playbook. Plan: three sections - Quick fixes, Snippet swaps, Canary checklist. I'll include 2 regexes for bulk replace and a sample smoke script.

InfraBot: Note: please enumerate exact /v1 path prefixes we need to redirect. Provide them in PR comment as a JSON list. Infra will create redirect map once PR merges.

Jordan Blake: I'll update Swift + Kotlin SDK READMEs. Suggest showing a compact before/after snippet and a one-liner about streaming callback rename.

Maya Iqbal: Small ask: include an "eval smoke" bullet in the checklist. We should run a few generation prompts in the canary to sanity-check semantics (not just errors). Add 5 representative prompts.

Leah Wong: Good call @Maya. I'll add an eval section: 5 prompts + expected rough outputs and a pass/fail guidance (semantic drift > 20% triggers rollback).

Kai Moreno: For the bulk-replace regexes, prefer conservative patterns. Example suggestions: 
- `s/\bmodel_name\b/model/g`
- `s/\bonData\b/onDelta/g`
Put them in the doc and explain risk (false matches).

source.text:
Nora Finch: opened a tiny PR for the 0.10.2 changelog. Main items: function-calling examples updated, one docs-only fix for link rot, and a note about a small regression in structured output formatting. PR: https://github.com/redwood-inference/docs/pull/4177

Alex Morgan: thanks. what's the structured output regression? severity?

Nora Finch: when using function/tool calling with streaming + JSON schema enforcement, nested arrays could be emitted with trailing commas causing strict parsers to fail. Low volume but bad for strict clients.

Priya Singh: ugh. we should give a short workaround and point to the SDK snippet showing tolerant parsing + recommended schema.

Nora Finch: suggested changelog blurb: "Known issue: some structured outputs emitted during streaming may include trailing commas, which can break strict JSON parsers. Workaround: disable streaming for function calls or use tolerant JSON parsing. Fix in next patch."

Ben Carter: please add a small JS snippet for tolerant parsing and the explicit non-streaming call. I can paste into the PR.

Priya Singh: snippet idea: show using the SDK plus JSON5 parsing fallback. Small two-line example so copy/paste works. Also add link to /guides/parsing/structured-output.md

Nora Finch: I'll add both: short snippet + link. Also noted a link-fix: old reference to /examples/old-func-call should instead go to /examples/function-calls/. Jen asked us to sweep those links — assigning to Docs Bot to remind.

Docs Bot: Reminder created: sweep links in changelog and examples. Assigned to Jen H.

Alex Morgan: add QA step in PR: run structured-output test suite with strict JSON parser enabled and streaming toggled off/on. That will catch regressions.

Ben Carter: added that test note to the PR description.

Priya Singh: wording tweak: shorten changelog line to "Temporary: streaming function outputs may include trailing commas; disable streaming or use tolerant parsing." Fits one-liner.

Nora Finch: good edit. tagging @Alex Morgan @Priya Singh for approvals.

Alex Morgan: approving.

Priya Singh: approving. Merge when CI passes. :shipit:

source.messages:
Elliot Price: Quick review pass on the workaround articles before we publish batch 1. Main theme: we need a *much* clearer “When to escalate” section + remove anything customer-identifying. Can folks skim and drop notes here?

Wei Chen: +1. I went through streaming timeouts + 429 quota mismatch. Both are solid technically, but the escalation guidance is kind of buried.
Wei Chen: Suggest we standardize a block at the end:
- When to escalate to Support
- What to include (request IDs, region, model, SDK version)
- What *not* to send (full prompts / PII)

Kira Thompson: I reviewed Dedicated autoscaling + Private installer upgrade. Private draft has a few lines that look like they came straight from a customer ticket (cluster name + exact timestamps). We should generalize those.

Dev Patel: Agree. Please treat these as “Support-safe / customer-facing” by default. If we need internal-only details, put them in the SUP ticket or the internal runbook, not the KB.

Priya Shah: On the “When to escalate” language: do we want hard thresholds or softer guidance? Like “if still failing after 3 retries” vs “if error rate >2%”?

Sean Gallagher: For public KB, avoid our internal paging thresholds. Use customer-observable signals.
Sean Gallagher: Example wording that works:
- “If the issue persists for >15 minutes despite the mitigations below”
- “If multiple regions are affected”
- “If you’re on Dedicated/Private and impact is production-blocking”
Sean Gallagher: And then Support can map that to P0/P1 internally.

Naomi Feldman: Also please scrub anything that could be considered sensitive operational detail:
- specific hostnames/IPs
- screenshots with account names
- copy/pasted logs that include tokens, emails, org IDs
Naomi Feldman: It’s fine to show *redacted* examples (e.g., `req_...`), but make the redaction explicit.

Elliot Price: Great. I’ll add a standard section template to each article:
1) Summary
2) Symptoms
3) Affected tiers/regions/models
4) Workarounds (step-by-step)
5) Verification (how to confirm it helped)
6) When to escalate
7) What to include in your Support request

Wei Chen: Streaming timeouts article: can we make the escalation callout more direct? Right now it says “contact Support if you need help” — too vague.
Wei Chen: Proposed:
“Escalate if: (a) streaming resets continue after setting a 60s idle timeout + retry with backoff, (b) you see consistent disconnects on a single region, or (c) you have request IDs showing server-side `stream_reset` errors.”

Dev Patel: +1, but remove “`stream_reset`” as a named internal classification unless customers already see it. Better: “errors indicating connection reset / incomplete chunked transfer” etc.

Kira Thompson: Dedicated autoscaling article: it mentions one customer’s exact config values (min=2, cooldown=90s) and calls it “Northstar recommended.” That has to go.
Kira Thompson: We can rewrite as ranges + explanation:
- “Start with min replicas = typical steady-state load (often 2–4)”
- “Set scale-down cooldown to at least 5–10 minutes if you see oscillation”
- “Escalate if latency SLOs are breached or scaling events >N per hour” (but phrase it customer-observable: “scaling events continuously for >30 minutes”).

Sean Gallagher: Yep. Keep it “if you see repeated scale up/down events every few minutes for >30m” rather than “>N/hour.”

Priya Shah: 429 / quota mismatch draft: it includes a snippet of a customer’s header values with their org ID embedded. I’ll replace with an anonymized example.

Naomi Feldman: Thank you. Also: avoid telling customers to disable security controls (e.g., “turn off TLS verification”). If there’s a debugging step like that, label it “DO NOT use in production” and ideally omit from KB.

Wei Chen: For the 429 article, we should add “Common misconfig” section:
- multiple concurrent retries causing burst
- not respecting `Retry-After`
- mixing keys across envs

Dev Patel: And include “What Support will ask for” so customers can pre-empt it:
- time window
- region
- endpoint
- request IDs
- SDK version
- whether they’re behind a proxy

Elliot Price: Action items I’m taking from this thread:
- Add a consistent “When to escalate” section to each article
- Remove customer names / configs / org IDs; only anonymized examples
- Align escalation language with customer-observable signals (no internal paging thresholds)
- Add “What to include” + safe log collection guidance

Kira Thompson: I’ll do another sweep specifically for Private upgrade article for any customer artifacts + make sure rollback guidance is framed as “contact Support before attempting rollback” for regulated customers.

Sean Gallagher: If you want one line that helps Support triage faster, add: “Include installer logs + timestamps” but don’t paste huge logs inline; attach via secure channel / Support portal.

Naomi Feldman: +1. Say “we may request logs via a secure upload link” so we’re not encouraging email attachments.

Wei Chen: ETA for updated drafts? I’m blocked on final copy for the SDK troubleshooting cross-link until these stabilize.

Elliot Price: I’ll push edits today and tag you both for a final sanity check. Goal is publish by tomorrow EOD so we can link from Support macros.

Dev Patel: Appreciate it. Once the “When to escalate” is consistent, Support can copy/paste without rewriting every time.

Elliot Price: Cool. I’ll also update the publishing checklist to include “customer-specific detail scrub” as a hard gate.

source.body:
Elliot Price: hey #docs — quick PR review request. We’ve got the enterprise security page updates ready to merge and I want eyes from Security/GRC + messaging before we ship.

Links:
- PR 2841 (enterprise security overview + gated SOC 2 request link stub): https://github.com/redwood-inference/redwood-docs/pull/2841
- PR 2849 (audit logging + export options): https://github.com/redwood-inference/redwood-docs/pull/2849
- PR 2860 (clarify data retention + residency language + glossary): https://github.com/redwood-inference/redwood-docs/pull/2860

Main asks:
1) confirm we’re not over-claiming anything (SOC2 / ISO / pen test wording)
2) confirm evidence request flow link + language is correct (gated, NDA)
3) sanity check Hosted vs Dedicated vs Private distinctions

If you can, please leave comments directly in the PRs by EOD tomorrow. :pray:

Kira Thompson: taking first pass on 2860 now. The glossary is super helpful.

Wei Chen: I’ll review 2841 + link behavior. Also will run the docs link checker locally.

Naomi Feldman: I can do a product-security alignment pass. Please flag any “roadmap-ish” language you think is borderline.

Marcus Lin: I’ll review security claims and IR/vuln mgmt phrasing. Can you point me to the specific sections where we mention pen tests / scanning?

Elliot Price: @Marcus Lin sure — in 2841 it’s under “Security program” + FAQ accordion. In 2849 there’s a short note about “continuous monitoring” that might need tightening.

redwood-docs-bot: CI checks started for PR #2860 (build, lint, link-check).

Daniel Carter: jumping on 2841. For SOC 2, we need to be careful about “certified” language. We can say “independently audited” and “SOC 2 Type II report available under NDA” (assuming that’s still accurate). I’ll comment in-line.

Camila Reyes: I’ll do messaging sweep (tone + competitive-safe). Heads up: avoid “bank-grade” / “military-grade” style claims.

Talia Benson: following — this is part of the kit refresh. Once merged, I’ll link these pages from the enablement kit index.

Wei Chen: link check update: the “Request SOC 2 report” button in 2841 currently points to `/trust/soc2` which 404s on preview.

Wei Chen: suggestion: either point to the evidence request playbook page (Confluence internal) for logged-in users, OR make the docs link go to a public “Contact sales / request trust docs” page and keep the actual SOC2 report gated behind the form.

Elliot Price: good catch. We don’t want public docs pointing to internal Confluence.

Elliot Price: I’ll swap it to the public intake route (we have the security@ alias + sales form). We can keep the exact evidence process in Confluence.

Kira Thompson: on 2860: one nit — the sentence “Redwood supports EU data residency” reads too absolute. The rest of the doc does a good job explaining regional availability and that some metadata may be processed outside the region depending on deployment mode.

Kira Thompson: maybe: “Redwood offers region selection in supported cloud regions. For EU residency requirements, Redwood Dedicated and Redwood Private can be deployed in EU regions; please confirm regional availability with your account team.”

Naomi Feldman: +1. Also for Hosted, we should avoid implying we can guarantee residency for *all* processing unless we truly can. Recommend anchoring it to “in-scope services” + “customer-selected region” and push strict residency to Dedicated/Private.

Daniel Carter: left comments on 2841 re: SOC 2 language. Proposed replacement:
- “Redwood maintains a SOC 2 Type II report covering the Redwood Inference platform. The report is available to customers and prospects under NDA upon request.”
Also please remove “SOC 2 certified” (not a thing).

Marcus Lin: reviewed 2841 + 2849.
- 2841: change “regular penetration testing” to “periodic penetration testing” unless we’re stating frequency. Questionnaires often ask for annual vs quarterly.
- 2849: the phrase “all admin actions are logged” is too broad. We should say “console and API administrative events are captured in audit logs” and refer to the list of event types.

Marcus Lin: also: avoid “real-time alerting” unless we ship alerting as a product feature. Export-to-SIEM is ok; alerts are on customer side.

Camila Reyes: did a sweep. Biggest thing: in 2841 there’s a line “we never retain prompts.” That’s not consistent with the retention language doc (we do have default retention windows + customer-configurable options in certain modes). Suggest changing to:
“By default, Redwood limits retention of customer content. Retention and logging behavior varies by deployment mode and configuration. See Data retention & deletion for details.”

Elliot Price: thank you. Updating now.

redwood-docs-bot: CI checks passed for PR #2860.

Elliot Price: pushed fixes:
- PR 2841: updated SOC 2 wording per @Daniel Carter, removed “certified,” changed pen-test language to “periodic,” updated “request trust docs” link to public form.
- PR 2849: tightened audit log language per @Marcus Lin (“captured events” vs “all actions”), removed “real-time alerting” phrasing.
- PR 2860: softened EU residency statement + made Hosted vs Dedicated/Private distinctions clearer.

Elliot Price: can folks re-check those sections and drop an approval if good?

Daniel Carter: re-checked 2841. SOC2 language looks good now. Approved. :thumbsup:

Marcus Lin: re-checked 2849 + 2841 changes. Looks aligned. Approved.

Naomi Feldman: skimmed final diff across all three. No roadmap promises. Looks consistent with product behavior as described. Approved.

Wei Chen: ran link checker locally — no broken links now. The “request trust docs” form resolves. Approved.

Kira Thompson: reviewed the updated residency/retention wording in 2860. This reads much safer + clearer. Approved.

Elliot Price: awesome, thanks all. Merging 2860 today, then 2849 + 2841 right after. Next step is updating the enablement kit index to point to these pages + adding release note in the FAQ changelog.

Talia Benson: :thumbsup: once merged, please send me the final URLs so we can update the kit tracker + HubSpot snippets.

Elliot Price: will do.

source.text:
Elliot Price: need quick alignment on marketplace onboarding docs structure/IA before we lock URLs for AWS/GCP submission forms. where do we want the nav entry + where do we link security + troubleshooting?

Alex Martinez: +1. partner portals want stable URLs and we keep getting asked “link to onboarding steps” + “link to security overview”. if we change paths post-submit it’s a mess.

Wei Chen: current draft is kind of scattered (Hosted quickstart vs marketplace quickstart vs pricing pages). i’d vote for a single “Marketplace” landing page with the flow, then split by AWS/GCP.

Kira Thompson: agree. also we need to handle Dedicated/Private buyers who come in via marketplace (not just Hosted). landing page should branch by “What you bought” (Hosted usage-based vs Dedicated capacity vs Private deployment).

Ben Carter: from funnel POV, keep step count low on landing page. the most common drop is “i subscribed… now what?” so above the fold should be 3–5 steps max, then expand.

Elliot Price: proposed IA (rough):
- Getting started
  - Marketplace (landing)
    - AWS Marketplace onboarding
    - GCP Marketplace onboarding
    - Troubleshooting (entitlements/provisioning/billing)
- Security
  - Marketplace security overview (public)
  - Data handling & retention (public-ish)
  - Audit logs (public-ish)
  - Evidence (gated / on request)

Thoughts?

Naomi Feldman: security pages: we can publish a “marketplace security overview” + shared responsibility model + high-level encryption/keys. BUT evidence links should NOT be public. we can point to “SOC 2 Type II available under NDA / via trust portal” wording.

Marcus Lin: +1. also be careful with “supports” vs “enforces” language on retention (configurable vs default). pls link to the retention doc but keep it explicit what’s default and what’s configurable.

Tessa Morgan: UX note: landing page should visually mirror console screens (Subscribe → Verify entitlement → Create/link org → Create API key → First request). even if docs are text-heavy, a simple diagram helps.

Jean-Paul Laurent: if we put “Marketplace” under Getting started, can we also add a callout on the generic “Quickstart” pages that says “Bought via AWS/GCP marketplace? start here” w/ link? otherwise people will follow old quickstart and then get confused.

Alex Martinez: yes, we should do a small “marketplace entrypoint” banner on quickstarts + on pricing page.

Wei Chen: for Hosted path, AWS/GCP guides should share 80% of the same steps. can we avoid duplicate content by having one “marketplace flow” page and then AWS/GCP pages that are mostly provider-specific bits (subscription screenshot + entitlement timing + where to find IDs)?

Elliot Price: we can do:
- /marketplace (overview + common flow)
- /marketplace/aws (AWS-specific: subscribe UI, customer identifier, private offer activation, entitlement propagation notes)
- /marketplace/gcp (GCP-specific: procurement steps, billing account selection, etc)
- /marketplace/troubleshooting (shared)

Kira Thompson: pls include “what plan did I buy?” section somewhere. example: Dedicated buyers need to know they still need to talk to SE to provision capacity / region + that marketplace subscription is the procurement layer, not the infra magically appearing.

Ben Carter: +1 but keep it crisp. maybe a “Choose your deployment” section with 3 tiles: Hosted / Dedicated / Private. each tile shows: what you get immediately vs what requires coordination.

Naomi Feldman: also link security overview from the landing page, but don’t make onboarding block on it. procurement folks will go hunting though—so prominent placement is good.

Marcus Lin: troubleshooting page should include entitlement timing expectations (e.g., “can take up to X minutes”), retry guidance, and what info to include in a support ticket (org id, marketplace provider, subscription id, region). keep it practical.

Alex Martinez: we should standardize the URLs we share with partners:
1) onboarding landing
2) security overview
3) troubleshooting
That’s what AWS asked for last time.

Elliot Price: what do we call the nav item? “Marketplace onboarding” vs just “Marketplace”? (trying not to bury it)

Wei Chen: nav label “Marketplace” is fine. H1 on landing can be “Marketplace onboarding (AWS & GCP)”.

Tessa Morgan: agree. “Marketplace” in nav is easy to scan.

Kira Thompson: for troubleshooting, can we split sections by symptom?
- I subscribed but Redwood says no entitlement
- I can’t link to an existing org
- I created an org but no API key page / permissions
- Billing dimension mismatch / unexpected line items

Ben Carter: yes please. and add “still stuck? contact support” with clear routing. marketplace customers always ask “AWS or Redwood?”

Naomi Feldman: + make sure support routing doesn’t imply AWS has access to our systems. “Contact Redwood support; AWS/GCP can only help with marketplace purchase mechanics.”

Marcus Lin: also add an “Incident/status” link if we have one. entitlement sync job issues will look like user error.

Alex Martinez: URLs / redirects: we have an internal request in flight for vanity URLs. i’d like to keep these stable:
- /docs/marketplace
- /docs/marketplace/aws
- /docs/marketplace/gcp
- /docs/marketplace/troubleshooting
- /docs/security/marketplace
If we need legacy redirects from old marketplace pages, now’s the time.

Elliot Price: ok. action items:
- Elliot: update IA in docs repo + nav + stubs with final URLs (today)
- Wei/Kira: confirm content owners per page (Hosted bits vs Dedicated/Private bits) (tomorrow)
- Naomi/Marcus: review wording for security links + retention/audit log claims (this week)
- Alex: coordinate redirects/vanity URL ticket + confirm with Partnerships which exact links go into AWS/GCP submission forms

Ben Carter: pls add a short “What happens after you subscribe?” section that includes entitlement delay expectations + a link to troubleshooting. reduces support load.

Elliot Price: :thumbsup: will do. I’ll post the updated sitemap once the PR is up.

Alex Martinez: thanks. once PR is live, i’ll share with Partnerships + PM for sign-off.

Marcus Lin: ping me on the PR. I want to double check we’re not over-promising on retention defaults.

Naomi Feldman: same. also let’s keep evidence references as “available upon request” and link to the security overview page rather than a dead-end.

Wei Chen: cool. i’ll start consolidating the common flow content so AWS/GCP pages are genuinely provider-specific.

Kira Thompson: and I’ll add the Dedicated/Private callouts + “what you get immediately” section.

Elliot Price: perfect. closing loop: nav = Getting started → Marketplace; Security section includes Marketplace security overview; troubleshooting is a first-class page under Marketplace. done.
